verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin (FN1) is a secreted glycoprotein/ECM component. The NCBI summary explicitly states it exists as a soluble dimeric form in plasma and as dimeric/multimeric forms 'at the cell surface and in extracellular matrix.' However, FN1 has no transmembrane domain, no GPI anchor, and no covalent linkage to a TM scaffold — it associates with the ECM and cell surface via reversible non-covalent interactions with integrins, heparan sulfate proteoglycans, and other ECM molecules. These interactions are wash-sensitive and FN1 remains in equilibrium with the soluble plasma pool. Pre-no checklist: (1) No cell-surface-directed therapeutic targets FN1 on the outer PM leaflet vs. soluble/matrix pool. (2) Not a TM precursor/sheddase substrate. (3) No wash-resistant covalent TM-partner tethering documented. (4) No documented stable minority PM pool; ECM/surface deposition is matrix-anchored, not PM-anchored. (5) No cancer/cell-death/activation-induced outer-leaflet ecto-pool documented for FN1 protein body itself. ECM deposition is excluded per the recruitment test and stable_surface_attachment exclusion clause. Verdict: secreted_only.
